refactor: introduce test-cases/, feature-board, and generic testing-agent-protocol

- Add docs/product/test-cases/ with individual files for S01-S11
  Each file has frontmatter (id, user_story, persona, depends_on) so the
  agent can discover and order scenarios without a hardcoded mapping table

- Add docs/product/feature-board.md (Obsidian Kanban)
  One card per user story; tracks build and test lifecycle
  Columns: Backlog / Building / In Testing / Awaiting Your Review / Done / Blocked

- Add docs/product/test-reports/README.md
  Index and naming convention for timestamped acceptance run reports

- Add docs/guides/testing-agent-protocol.md (generic)
  Replaces manual-acceptance-testing.md; protocol only — no hardcoded
  scenario list, no hardcoded story IDs, no hardcoded host names

- Delete docs/guides/manual-acceptance-testing.md (superseded)
  Content split to test-cases/ (scenarios) and testing-agent-protocol.md (protocol)

- Update docs/README.md to reflect new structure

- Add .obsidian/ and Untitled Kanban.md to .gitignore

commit.message:
refactor: Major project cleanup - CI fix and professionalization

**DELETED** (removed debug/temp files):
- FINAL_CLEANUP.bat, cleanup_final.py, execute_tasks.py
- test_stdin_bridge.py, run_stdin_test.bat
- Redundant docs (MASTER_INSTRUCTIONS.md, PHASE_2_ROADMAP.md,
  PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md, PROJECT_ORGANIZATION.md)
- validation/outputs/ (temporary test results)

**MOVED** (fixed CI issue):
- requirements.txt: config/ → root
- requirements-dev.txt: config/ → root
- Reason: CI/CD systems expect these at project root

**UPDATED**:
- .github/workflows/ci.yml: Removed requirements-dev.txt install
  (keep CI simple; only production deps needed)

**ADDED** (quick reference for users):
- PROJECT_STATUS.txt: One-page guide (simple English, no jargon)
- CLEANUP_SUMMARY.txt: Record of all changes
- README_CLEANUP.txt: Human-friendly explanation

**RESULT**: Project is now:
✅ Clean (no debug files)
✅ Professional (organized, well-structured)
✅ CI-Ready (requirements in correct location)
✅ User-Friendly (clear quick reference guide)
✅ Production-ready (ready to share publicly)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add handoff normalizer, pre-compute architect context, fix stateless first-turn bloat

- Add _normalize_handoff_from_text(): reconstructs ---HANDOFF--- block from
  loose fields in agent response, avoiding costly retry re-sends
- Add _build_state_summary(): enriches architect's state with task counts,
  ready queue, and planning sufficiency — saves frontier tokens on mechanical
  analysis
- Fix stateless mode first_turn: use per-agent first_turn_done set instead of
  always treating stateless as first_turn (prevents full prompt re-injection
  every turn)
- Add 4 tests for handoff normalizer (loose fields, missing fields, parseability)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

analyzer: add net/http ReadRequest sentinel mapping

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Cache resolved edits for replay: avoid re-materializing on every replay

ensureCachedApplied() builds and caches the resolved edit list from
a full materialization. resolveReplayEdit() now scans the cached
list instead of replaying the entire history. Cache is invalidated
alongside cachedOrder when events are added or compacted.

Reduces cost of N replays from O(N * M) materializations to
1 materialization + N * O(S) scans (S = structural edits).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: deduplicate participant counts by personId in hub and home cards

Congregation and supervision participant counts now use unique personId
sets instead of raw cell_members doc count. Fixes inflated numbers when
a person belongs to multiple cells in the same congregation/supervision.

Affected: home_tab (supervision card, congregation card, admin list),
supervision_hub_screen, congregation_hub_screen.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: crop, max resolution, and watermark support

- Backend: ProcessingOptions struct (CropRect, WatermarkOptions, WatermarkPosition)
- Backend: build_video_filters() + build_gif_filters() helper for FFmpeg chains
- Backend: extract_frame/frames and generate_gif now accept ProcessingOptions
- Backend: GET /api/settings - returns watermarkLocked + defaultWatermark from env
- Backend: POST /api/session/{id}/watermark - PNG upload (max 5 MB, PNG-header validated)
- Backend: resolve_watermark_image() - per-session upload → env fallback chain
- Backend: export and gif handlers accept processing options from request body
- Backend: ttf-dejavu added to Alpine Dockerfile for drawtext font support
- Frontend: useSettings composable - fetches /api/settings on editor mount
- Frontend: useImageOptions composable - crop/maxWidth/watermark state + toProcessingOptions()
- Frontend: CropOverlay.vue - draggable + resizable normalised crop rectangle on video
- Frontend: WatermarkPositionPicker.vue - 3x3 grid for 9 positions
- Frontend: ImageOptionsPanel.vue - collapsible crop/resolution/watermark sidebar panel
- Frontend: useExport + useGif now forward ProcessingOptions to backend
- Frontend: editor.vue wired with CropOverlay, ImageOptionsPanel, useSettings
- Env: .env.example documents all FRAMESNAP_WATERMARK_* variables
- Tests: 41 frontend tests, 20 Rust tests, all green

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent stale duration_ms propagation on track change

- Add _actual_duration_ms field populated from stream_details.duration
- Add _best_duration_ms() helper preferring actual over Ynison state
- Reset duration_ms to 0 in sent state when advancing/going to previous
- Use _best_duration_ms() in all playback callbacks (_on_play/pause/seek)
- _advance_queue returns bool: True=next found, False=exhausted
- Exhausted queue sets _stream_stop_event for clean stream stop
- Update _update_metadata to prefer actual duration
- Add 3 new tests: actual duration preference, exhausted with actual, best_duration

Fixes premature track stop caused by stale duration from previous track.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

AZOPS-30 feat: sync workflow templates with repo template updates (#3)

## Summary
- Pin all GitHub Actions to commit SHAs across all workflow templates
- Update auto-review to check all PR statuses (CI + CodeQL) before
approving
- Migrate auto-review from `app-id`/`@v2` to `client-id`/`@v3`
- Update commit lint pattern to support multiple Jira keys
- Remove `dependency-review-action` (requires GHAS, replaced by Code
Security)
- Move inline `${{ }}` refs to `env:` vars in `run:` blocks for security
- Align PR template and CONTRIBUTING.md with template repo

## Test Plan
- [ ] CI passes (YAML/JSON validation)
- [ ] Workflow templates render correctly in Actions > New workflow
- [ ] Auto-review bot approves this PR

## Checklist
- [x] No secrets, credentials, or sensitive data committed
- [x] Changes match the intent of the linked Jira issue
- [x] Large binary assets use Git LFS
- [x] PR is under 400 lines (or justification provided)

## AI Disclosure
- [x] This PR contains AI-assisted code
- Tool(s) used: Claude Code (Opus 4.6)

commit.message:
docs: Add Phase 2 Roadmap for professional excellence (10/10 grade)

Create PHASE_2_ROADMAP.md outlining professional enhancement plan:

Current Phase 1: 7/10 grade (hardware-ready, functional, validated)
Target Phase 2: 10/10 grade (automotive-grade professional standard)

9 professional tasks over 40-50 hours:
- PEP 8 & type hints for code quality
- Extended Kalman Filter for firmware optimization
- Collision probability analytics
- PDF safety audit reporting
- Dashboard UI/UX enhancements
- 90%+ test coverage expansion
- CI/CD automation with quality gates
- ISO 15623 safety compliance documentation
- Protocol verification process

Includes: Priority breakdown, implementation timeline (3-4 weeks),
resource requirements, risk mitigation, success metrics, dependency
graph, decision framework.

Establishes roadmap for team to maintain 10/10 professional grade.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: Add Master Instructions for professional 10/10 maintenance standard

Create MASTER_INSTRUCTIONS.md defining professional maintenance practices:

- Task 1: PEP 8 & type hints (pylint ≥9.5/10)
- Task 2: Extended Kalman Filter for firmware
- Task 3: Collision probability calculation & analytics
- Task 4: Dashboard UI/UX enhancements (risk gauge, safety score)
- Task 5: PDF report generator for safety audits
- Task 6: Test coverage expansion (≥90%)
- Task 7: CI/CD automation with quality gates
- Task 8: Safety compliance documentation (ISO 15623)
- Task 9: Protocol verification (immutable 7-field contract)

Includes: Standards (ISO 15623, PEP 8, MISRA-C), acceptance criteria,
timelines, deployment checklist, rapid response fixes, professional
handoff template.

Establishes authoritative reference for maintaining 10/10 grade.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: add ConvNEF pipeline to technical report

Add Section 3.11 describing the gradient-free convolutional pipeline
architecture: PCA-based filter learning, multi-scale parallel stages,
spatial pyramid pooling, feature standardization, and ensemble
training.  All components are fully gradient-free.

Add Section 5.10 with comprehensive CIFAR-10 results from 7 sweep
rounds (80+ configurations on a T4 GPU).  Key results:
- PCA conv features lift flat-pixel 50.1% to 63.2% (+13.1%)
- Feature standardization is the largest single lever (+4.2%)
- Multi-scale parallel stages add +2.5% over single-scale
- 10-member ensemble with 30k neurons reaches 78.25%
- K-means filters, hierarchical stages, cutout, rectangular
  patches, and GCN all identified as negative results

Update Abstract to mention the 78.25% ConvNEF result.
Update Discussion 6.1 with ConvNEF competitive positioning.
Update strategy table (6.2) with ConvNEF row.
Update CIFAR-10 limitation (6.3) to reflect 78.3% vs prior 58%.
Add finding 13 to Conclusions covering ConvNEF.
Add Coates et al. (2011) reference for k-means filter comparison.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add fresh-seed checkbox to Optimize UI

- New 'Fresh naming' checkbox in Optimize.jsx lets users run the AI
  with a clean slate — skips seeding Phase 1 with old parent names.
- Default (unchecked) preserves the current behavior: seed from all
  49 previously-used parent names for naming consistency.
- fresh_seed bool flows from frontend checkbox → api.js → POST /ai/optimize
  body → start_optimize → _run_optimize_task → run_optimize(fresh_seed=).
- optimizer.py: when fresh_seed=True in full mode, initial_parent_names
  is set to [] and a 'Fresh-seed mode' log line is emitted.
- Bump to 0.3.26.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: fail fast when DOCKER_HOST points to an external daemon (workflow-scope DinD) (#1909)

* Initial plan

* feat: detect workflow-scope DinD (DOCKER_HOST) and fail fast

Add checkDockerHost() to src/cli.ts that inspects DOCKER_HOST on startup.
If it points at a non-default socket (e.g. tcp://localhost:2375 for a DinD
sidecar), AWF exits immediately with a clear error explaining why it is
incompatible and pointing at the new docs section.

Also add a "Workflow-Scope DinD Incompatibility" section to docs/usage.md
documenting the root cause, the error message users will see, and the
--enable-dind workaround for agents that genuinely need Docker access.

commit.message:
Fix ramp-up: open connections on main thread before launching user task

- Connections now opened serially on the main thread (not in parallel tasks)
- Each user's connection is fully open before the next user starts
- Ramp delay subtracts connection time so total ramp stays on schedule
- ActiveUsers counter now accurately reflects connected users
- User query loop runs on background task with pre-opened connections

commit.message:
analyzer: infer io.Reader invoke as EOF

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace BearSSL amalgamation with git submodule

The amalgamation approach failed due to ~20+ static symbol collisions
(K, block_size, P256_P, point_add, ccert_vtable, etc.) that BearSSL
assumes are file-local in separate compilation units.

Now using BearSSL as a proper git submodule:
- bearssl/ submodule from https://www.bearssl.org/git/BearSSL
- Taskfile: build_bearssl_lib() compiles all .c files -> libbearssl.a
  per architecture (build/, build_arm/, build_aarch64/, build_riscv/)
- l_tls.h: #include bearssl/inc/bearssl.h (public headers only)
- ci.ps1: git submodule update --init before Linux/ARM builds
- Non-TLS binaries are completely unaffected (no BearSSL linking)

CI: 10/10 targets pass (Win, Lin gcc+clang, ARM32 gcc+clang, AArch64 gcc+clang)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
